input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's a complete `AppShell.xaml` setup:

**AppShell.xaml**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: two sub-tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent Title="Active"
                          Icon="icon_active.png"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}" />
            <ShellContent Title="Archived"
                          Icon="icon_archived.png"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**
```csharp
namespace MyMauiApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register route for detail page (not in the visual hierarchy)
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Views.ProductDetailsPage),
                              typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating to the detail page** (e.g. from `ActiveProductsPage`):

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(ProductDetailsPage),
    new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Product"] = selectedProduct });
```

Receive the parameter with `[QueryProperty]`:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(Product), "Product")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public Product Product { get; set; }
}
```

**Key points:**

- **`FlyoutItem` → `Tab` → `ShellContent`** is the hierarchy. A `FlyoutItem` with a single `ShellContent` shows one page; wrapping multiple `ShellContent` items in a `<Tab>` gives you bottom tabs within that flyout entry.
- **`ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate`** delays page creation until it's navigated to.
- **`Routing.RegisterRoute`** is needed for pages *not* declared in the Shell visual tree (like detail pages you push onto the navigation stack).
- Navigation uses **relative URI** (`GoToAsync("ProductDetailsPage")`) since the route was registered globally, not as an absolute path.
